'''Windu's Guile''' was a rare variant type of [[Hurrikaine crystal]] that was named in honor of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], a member of the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] during the last [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Such [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|crystals]] were [[Color/Legends|violet]] in color and could be used in the construction of a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], the traditional weapon of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], crystals of the Windu's Guile variety were used by [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]] fighting for both the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].

==Properties==
Windu's Guile was a variant type of [[Hurrikaine crystal]],<ref name="SWGTCG" /> a type of crystal that was given to a young [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] named [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] by the [[Unidentified Hurikane species|natives]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Hurikane]] after Windu [[Force healing/Legends|healed]] one of them.<ref name="Stones">This article assumes that the "Hurrikaine crystal" in the ''Star Wars Galaxies Trading Card Game'' is the same type as the crystals from the planet Hurikane in the comic "[[Stones]]." This is based of the fact that their colors are the same, both are linked to Mace Windu, and that the ''[[Jedi Academy Training Manual]]'' says that Hurikane crystals are from the planet Hurikane.</ref> The crystals were polyhedral in structure, had facets of differing size and shape, and could be found as large as an individual's fist. Windu's Guile crystals were [[Color/Legends|violet]]–colored. The crystals could be used in the construction of a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]<ref name="SWGTCG" />—the traditional weapon of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]<ref name="SWEIV">{{Film|IV}}</ref> of [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] peacekeepers<ref name="SWEI">{{Film|I}}</ref>—and produced a brilliant violet–colored blade.<ref name="SWGTCG" /> A [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-sensitive individual could tune the crystal for use in lightsaber construction. The tuning process caused it to become a focusing crystal that could only be effectively used in a lightsaber constructed by the individual who tuned it.<ref name="SWG" />

==History==
The rare Hurrikaine crystal variant known as Windu's Guile was named to honor Mace Windu,<ref name="SWGTCG" /> who had gone on to become a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] and a member of the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] during the final [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="SWEIII">{{Film|III}}</ref> The crystals were found in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], which was fought between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]].<ref name="SWG" />

{{Gamemechanics}}
The crystals were used by Force-sensitive individuals fighting for both sides during the war after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].<ref name="SWG" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
Windu's Guile appeared in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> In the game, when the crystal was installed in a lightsaber, it gave the weapon a cold damage bonus.<ref name="SWG" />

Windu's Guile was part of a series of 19 unique lightsaber color crystals created in the context of the [[W:c:swg:Publish 9 Notes for 29 June 04|Publish 9]]'s Jedi system revamp<ref name="SWG:js">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=other|jedi_spam.stf}}</ref> in [[June]] [[2004]].<ref name="P9">{{SonyCite|url=recent_updates.jsp?page=Recent%20Updates|text=Publish 9 Secrets of the Force}}</ref> However, only one of those crystals — [[Sunrider's Destiny]] — was made available in the game.<ref name="SWG:CEL">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=general|Capture Enemy Leadership|Dathomir}}</ref> Windu's Guile and the other crystals were not introduced in the game at that time.<ref name="soe.lithium">{{SonyForumsCite|full_url=http://soe.lithium.com/swg/board/message?board.id=jedi&message.id=1286265|text=A perfect way to (re)introduce all named color crystals and flawless pearls: Chu-Gon Dar Cube!|archiveurl=https://www.swgemu.com/archive/scrapbookv51/data/20070213155714/index.html|nolive=1}}</ref><ref name="thehonorempire">{{WebCite|url=https://www.thehonorempire.org/web/forums/topic/8045-rare-color-crystal-colors|text=Rare Color Crystal Colors|work=[https://www.thehonorempire.org/ Honor Empire Community]|archivedate=20250801192732}}</ref> Windu's Guile was eventually added to the game with the release of the the "[[W:c:swg:Chapter 3|Chapter 3]]," on [[September 27]], [[2006]].<ref name="CA2006">{{SonyCite|url=players/content.vm?id=64186&resource=publish|text=Chapter Archive 2006}}</ref> The crystal was only available to players of the Jedi profession working for the Rebellion or Empire who achieved the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[General/Legends|general]]. Once having achieved this rank, the player was then able to purchase the crystal from a recruiter for a base price of 1 million credits.<ref name="SWG" /> Eventually, on [[October 20]], [[2011]], the [[W:c:swg:Hotfix 21.4|Hotfix 21.4]] added the crystal as a [[W:c:swg:Rare Loot System|lootable]] reward from enemies defeated during "[[W:c:swg:Hotfix 21.3|raids]],"<ref name="21.4">{{SonyCite|url=players/news_archive.vm?id=69225&month=102011|text=Star Wars Galaxies Hotfix 21.4 -- Thursday, October 20, 2011}}</ref> which allowed players greater access to the crystal.<ref name="SWG" />

In [[2008]], Windu's Guile received a card in [[Champions of the Force (SWGTCG)|''Champions of the Force'']], the first set for the virtual ''[[Star Wars Galaxies Trading Card Game]]'', released by Sony Online Entertainment to go along with ''Star Wars Galaxies''. The Windu's Guile card was illustrated by artist [[Kuki Huang]].<ref name="SWGTCG" />
